steps.title: Clear the fraction
steps.content:
Start with the given equation:
$$ak = \frac{b}{15}(6+m)$$
Since $b$ is positive, multiply both sides by $\frac{15}{b}$ to isolate the parenthetical expression:
$$\frac{15ak}{b} = 6+m$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $m=\frac{15(ak-6)}{b}$ — incorrect; this would require subtracting $6$ before multiplying by $\frac{15}{b}$, but the $6$ should be subtracted AFTER multiplying by $\frac{15}{b}$
  • B: $m=ak-6-\frac{b}{15}$ — incorrect; the $\frac{b}{15}$ should be inverted to $\frac{15}{b}$ and multiplied by $ak$, not subtracted
  • C: $m=ak-\frac{6b}{15}$ — incorrect; same error as B, the fraction was not properly cleared by multiplying by its reciprocal
  • D: $m=\frac{15ak}{b}-6$ — matches our derivation exactly
That confirms D is correct.
